# Project Decisions & Context

<!-- Regenerate with: npx project-memory sync -->

## Architecture
- Portfolio calculations use transactions-only, holdings for metadata — portfolioService.ts rewritten to derive holdings/summary/returns entirely from investment_transactions + latest NAV. investment_holdings table is still written (archive) but never read for calculations. Period view uses cohort math: invested-in-period, current value of those units, XIRR over window cash flows. ALL = lifetime.
- AI feature uses user-provided API keys with multi-provider support — Users provide their own API keys (Claude/Gemini/OpenAI). Keys stored locally on device (SecureStore). App calls LLM APIs directly with tool_use for financial queries over local SQLite data. No backend proxy needed. Tool layer structured for future MCP server extraction.
- AI v1 scope: Gemini provider, model selection, token cost display, no history — v1 starts with Gemini only. Users pick model (Flash/Pro). No conversation persistence — fresh each session. Show token cost on screen (input/output tokens × model pricing). Tool layer is provider-agnostic for future Claude/OpenAI addition.
- MyFinance Web re-platform: Node/TS monorepo, Claude Agent SDK, MCP, SQLite→Postgres via Drizzle — Re-platforming the local-first RN app into a self-hosted (single-user, auth later) wealth-manager. Stack locked: Node+TypeScript backend (ports existing features/ as-is); Claude Agent SDK for the agent orchestrator + specialist agents; MCP server as the tool contract over finance data; Drizzle ORM targeting SQLite now → Postgres+pgvector later (RAG vectors in same DB). Monorepo: packages/core (ported features/ + schema + types), packages/api, packages/mcp, packages/agents, apps/web, apps/mobile. The AI agent layer is the product; backend/UI/MCP exist to feed it. Bottom-up build order L0 Foundation → L1 Ingestion+API → L2 Web UI → L3 MCP → L4 AI. Full plan: docs/superpowers/MASTER_PLAN.md.
- L0 keeps money as REAL/float (paise rejected) and uses repository-over-Drizzle seam — L0 Foundation decisions (spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-l0-foundation-design.md): (1) Money stays REAL/float, faithful port — integer-paise rejected because for single-user/single-portfolio the only benefit (exact aggregates) is cosmetic (hidden by toFixed(2)) and a rupee->paise rounding step would risk diverging from already-Groww-validated numbers. Revisit as clean migration if drift bites. (2) Data layer = repository modules own ALL Drizzle/SQL; pure domain/ logic (xirr, returns, portfolio, categorize) depends on injected repo INTERFACES, never Drizzle — enables fake-repo unit tests + isolates Postgres swap to client.ts+schema.ts. (3) Scope shift: import parsers+orchestration ported into core in L0; L1 becomes the server-side ingestion API over them. (4) better-sqlite3 driver isolated in db/client.ts. (5) Fastify read-only API skeleton. Build via subagent-driven-development on branch layer/0-foundation.
- L0 returns/portfolio port unifies XIRR terminal-flow date on LOCAL time (formatDate), server TZ matters — Task 3.2 port: the RN originals were inconsistent — portfolioService used UTC (new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)) for terminal XIRR cash-flow dates, while returnsCalculator.getPeriodReturns used LOCAL time (formatDate via getFullYear/getMonth/getDate). The port (packages/core/src/domain/{returns,portfolio}.ts) unified on LOCAL formatDate everywhere. RATIONALE: getPeriodReturns is the function under the Groww golden-master test, so preserving its local-time behavior exactly is correct; portfolioService's UTC was the inconsistency. Spec-review (feature-dev:code-reviewer) confirmed all other 16 logic points are faithful ports. KNOWN ITEM (not a bug): local-time formatDate means cash-flow/terminal dates depend on the SERVER's timezone. For this India-based single-user wealth manager, deploy with TZ=Asia/Kolkata (IST) so dates match the user's expectation and Groww. Impact on XIRR is sub-day/immaterial over year-scale windows regardless. `today` is injectable (default new Date()) for deterministic tests. Revisit if multi-TZ ever matters. Decision id from subagent: 2198ce4c (its own save).
- Categorization engine ported to packages/core/src/domain/categorize.ts (pure verbatim + repo-injected sync) — Ported categorizeTransaction.ts + manageRules.ts into categorize.ts. Pure matchers (extractMerchantKey/extractUpiNoteKeyword/createCategorizationInput/resolveCategoryFromRules/builtinRules + helpers) are byte-identical to source; only change is resolveCategoryFromRules reads camelCase StoredCategoryRule fields (ruleType/patternValue/categoryId) instead of snake_case. DB functions (recategorizeNonManualTransactions/saveCategoryMemoryRule/createRule/updateRuleCategory/deleteRule) take injected {ruleRepo,txRepo}; synchronous (better-sqlite3). recategorize re-derives input from description (not stored merchant_key/upi columns), matching source. Extended CategoryRuleRepo.updateRuleCategory(ruleId,categoryId,ruleType?,priority?) in types.ts+categoryRuleRepo.ts to faithfully set rule_type+priority(merchant?200:100). Category CRUD (create/rename/delete) DEFERRED — needs CategoryRepo extension, noted in file. Commit ae7e0b0 on layer/0-foundation. 119 tests green, typecheck clean.
- Import orchestration ported to packages/core with injected runInTransaction runner + injectable amfiMatch — Task 5.2: the 3 import orchestrators (importTransactions HDFC expense, importHoldings, importInvestmentTransactions) now live in packages/core/src/import/, repo-injected (no drizzle/schema imports). Transactions: each fn takes deps.runInTransaction<T>(fn)=>T built by the caller from the better-sqlite3 handle's .transaction(); per-row insert loops + deletes run inside it for atomicity. importTransactions and importInvestmentTransactions are synchronous-bodied but return Promise (signature parity); importHoldings stays truly async because the post-import AMFI auto-match is network. AMFI network call is an injectable dep amfiMatch?({schemeRepo}) defaulting to domain/amfiMatcher.autoMatchAmfiCodes; tests inject a stub returning {matched:0,total:0,matches:[]} so no network. settleDelayMs (default 1000) also injectable. New repo methods added: HoldingsRepo.deleteByAccountAppDate, InvestmentTxRepo.deleteByAccountAppDateRange, ImportHistoryRepo.findInvestmentImports/deleteInvestmentImport, ExpenseTransactionRepo.insertIgnore (onConflictDoNothing == INSERT OR IGNORE on UNIQUE dedupe_key). createInvestmentImport already supported holdings totals — no extension needed. Source strategy preserved verbatim: expense import inserts import_history + reads rules OUTSIDE the tx then loops inside; holdings find-existing/delete-holdings/delete-history/insert-new/match-or-create order; investment-tx pre-flight scheme resolution returns unmatched_schemes (sorted) WITHOUT touching DB. 167 core tests pass, typecheck clean. Commit 7523a85.
- packages/api L0: Fastify read-only API wires repos+domain via direct decoration; nav from core, no-amfi fixtures keep tests offline — packages/api (L0 read-only skeleton) is built. buildServer(opts) returns a non-listening Fastify instance (logger:false) used in tests via app.inject(). DB wiring: registerDb(app,dbPath) calls core runMigrations(dbPath) and decorates app.db/app.sqlite/app.repos DIRECTLY (not app.register) so decorations live on the root instance (avoids encapsulation scoping); onClose hook closes sqlite. FastifyInstance is type-augmented (declare module 'fastify') with db/sqlite/repos; sqlite type is derived as ReturnType<typeof runMigrations>['sqlite'] to avoid a direct better-sqlite3 dep in api. Endpoints: GET /health -> {data:{status:'ok',db}} (SELECT 1 probe); GET /transactions?limit&offset&categoryId -> {data:rows} via expenseTxRepo.list; GET /investments/summary -> {data:summary} via getPortfolioSummary({txRepo,nav}); GET /investments/returns?period -> {data:returns} via getPeriodReturns({txRepo,schemeRepo,holdingsRepo,nav}), invalid period throws Error w/ statusCode=400. Error handler returns {error:{message,statusCode}} (default 500, respects err.statusCode). NAV dependency is built from core getLatestNAV/getNAVForDate but is NEVER called in tests because fixtures seed schemes with NULL amfi_code -> domain falls back to invested/holdings value (offline, deterministic). NO write/import endpoints (that's L1).
- L0 Foundation COMPLETE + PR #1 opened; L1 (ingestion API) is next layer — L0 Foundation layer DONE 2026-06-12. Branch layer/0-foundation (29 commits) pushed to origin (ak688744/MyFinance); PR #1 open into main: https://github.com/ak688744/MyFinance/pull/1. MASTER_PLAN §4 L0 row = ✅.

DELIVERED (packages/api HTTP layer over core, NO new package):
- plugins/txRunner.ts: makeRunInTransaction(app.sqlite) => <T>(fn)=>T (sqlite.transaction(fn)()), feeds core import orchestrators.
- lib/multipart.ts: readMultipart(req) => {file:{buffer:ArrayBuffer,filename}|null, fields} via @fastify/multipart@^9 (first-file-wins; buffer sliced into standalone ArrayBuffer for XLSX type:'array'). server.ts registers multipart with limits {fileSize:25MB, files:1}.
- import/registry.ts: resolveParser(platform, kind) — REGISTRY {hdfc:{expense:parseHdfcStatementXls}, groww:{holdings:parseGrowwHoldingsXls, transactions:parseGrowwTransactionXls}}; case-insensitive platform; BARE function refs (test asserts .toBe); unsupported pair throws statusCode-400. D6 seam — ET Money etc. drop-in with zero route/orchestrator change.
- routes/imports.ts: POST /imports/expenses (platform default hdfc), /imports/investments/holdings, /imports/investments/transactions (default groww) + GET /imports. D1 server-parses-upload; parser throw caught->400; missing file/field->400. holdings/tx REQUIRE accountName+investmentApp (D7). investment-tx returns BOTH {status:'success',...} and {status:'unmatched_schemes',unmatchedSchemes[]} as 200 (D2). Wiring: expenses uses expenseTxRepo+categoryRuleRepo; investment-tx uses txRepo (investment); amfiMatch injected (defaults core autoMatchAmfiCodes).
- routes/categories.ts: GET /categories; POST(201)/PATCH/DELETE /categories/rules; POST /recategorize. createRule empty-pattern->400 (core throws 'Rule pattern cannot be empty.'). create/update/delete each re-run recategorize internally (core behavior, no extra calls).
- routes/investments.ts EXPANDED: GET /investments/holdings (+?account, sortBy, sortOrder), /allocation (+?account), /accounts (distinct names, D7). getHoldings/getAssetAllocation async; getAccounts sync.
- buildServer({dbPath, amfiMatch}) — amfiMatch:AmfiMatch (type exported from routes/imports.ts) lets tests inject no-network stub returning {matched:0,total:0}. registerDb now calls seedDatabase(db) so starter categories exist on fresh DB.

ONE core change (non-financial, NO Groww re-validation): ImportHistoryRepo.listAll() in repositories/importHistoryRepo.ts + ImportRecord type in repositories/types.ts (also now exported from core index.ts). Unifies import_history (expense) + investment_import_history rows into ImportRecord[] {kind:'expense'|'investment', id, sourceName, importType, recordCount, accountName, investmentApp, importedAt}, JS-side sort importedAt desc -> kind -> id. No XIRR/returns/portfolio/categorize/NAV math touched.

ORGANIZING INSIGHT — THREE STRUCTURAL FAMILIES (not 2):
- Family 1 (transactional/market): MF now (stocks DEFERRED). Transaction stream, units×price, XIRR. Stays in existing investment_schemes/investment_transactions; portfolio.ts UNCHANGED. Stocks will EXTEND this pipeline (scheme→instrument), NEVER the assets table.
- Family 2 (single-value): FD (computed, one deposit) + gold/real_estate/cash (manual stated value).
- Family 3 (contributory/computed): PPF/EPF/NPS — RECURRING contributions to same account, no units/NAV, rate may change over time. User flagged this: FD is one-time but PPF/EPF/NPS keep receiving deposits.

LOCKED DECISIONS (D1-D11):
- D1 v1 scope: implement MF(market) + manual(gold/RE/cash) + computed(FD/PPF/EPF/NPS) + loans(amortization). DEFER live stock/gold-ETF pricing (no new price provider). assetClass enum OPEN.
- D2 MF data path: keep MF tables as-is, portfolio.ts XIRR math UNCHANGED — lowest Groww re-validation risk. Net-worth rollup UNIONS MF path + new classes.
- D3 global assets surface: READ-TIME UNIFIED PROJECTION (getAllAssets) — physical assets table is Family 2/3 only; MF is projected in at read time (MF value/units/XIRR always derived, never stored, so no MF rows in assets — avoids duplication+staleness+import-sync). User explicitly asked if MF/stocks fit in assets table; answer: MF projected, stocks extend MF pipeline.
- D4 net-worth history: DERIVE-ON-READ (no snapshot table). Current NW = must-have; trend series = STRETCH. MF history needs getNAVForDate (already exists on NavLookup); manual assets need asset_valuations time-series; computed deterministic by formula.
- D5 new-math validation: TEXTBOOK-FORMULA unit tests (EMI/amortization, compound-interest/FD-maturity). No external oracle for these. Groww golden-master still guards MF/XIRR.
- D6 rate over time: RATE SCHEDULE (asset_rates: effective_from+rate periods); compound each contribution under the rate in effect per period. FD=1 period; PPF multi (govt resets).
- D7 contribution shape: asset_contributions stream. FD=1 row, PPF/EPF/NPS=N rows, manual=0.
- D8 accounts: first-class, PER-DOMAIN (investment/expense), two-level (institution,label) so MULTIPLE GROWW ACCOUNTS = same institution distinct label (matches existing accountName/investmentApp grouping in portfolio.ts GroupKey). INCLUDES expense accounts this layer (nullable account_id on transactions).
- D9 AI insight slots DEFERRED to L4. D10 budgets DEFERRED to L2.
- D11 NO data migration (no prod data yet) — pure schema evolution: 6 new tables (accounts, assets, asset_contributions, asset_rates, asset_valuations, liabilities) + transactions.account_id. Groww gate = standing re-validation INVARIANT not data-preservation.

NEW TABLES: accounts, assets(account_id, asset_class CHECK, valuation_strategy computed|manual, ingestion_mode, params JSON, status), asset_contributions, asset_rates, asset_valuations, liabilities(principal/annual_rate/tenure_months|emi_amount/start_date, CHECK tenure-or-emi). Money/rate/value all REAL (L0 decision). New domain modules: domain/valuation/{computed,manual,index}.ts, domain/loans/amortization.ts, domain/networth/networth.ts (getAllAssets/getNetWorth/getNetWorthHistory, repo+getMfHoldings injected). New repos: account/asset/assetContribution/assetRate/assetValuation/liability. New API routes: /networth(+/history), /assets(+/:id/contributions|valuations|rates), /accounts, /liabilities; MF import ensureAccount (non-financial).

T1 GATE: existing Groww golden-master + full core suite pass UNCHANGED (MF pipeline untouched) + new rollup-continuity test (MF-only getNetWorth.totalAssets == getPortfolioSummary.totalCurrentValue). After L1.5 -> L2 (Web UI). Related decisions: L1.5 model/sequencing 437133fd, frontend spike c5a4e601, L1 complete ab012766.
- L1.5 Task 3: domain types appended to packages/core/src/types.ts (Account/Asset/Liability/ValuedAsset/NetWorth*) — L1.5 Task 3 added all unified-investment domain types to the END of packages/core/src/types.ts (appended, no existing type modified; zero name collisions verified before edit). Types: AccountDomain, Account, AssetClass (ppf/epf/nps/fd/gold/real_estate/cash), ValuationStrategy (market|computed|manual), IngestionMode (manual_entry|file_import), CompoundingFrequency, AssetParams (JSON params with compounding/maturityDate/payout/grams + index signature), Asset (valuationStrategy narrowed to computed|manual — market is MF-only/projected, never an assets-table row), AssetContribution, AssetRate (annual %), AssetValuation, Liability (loanType home|car|personal|other, tenureMonths|emiAmount nullable), ValuedAsset (uniform projection shape; assetId null for MF, assetClass 'mutual_fund' for MF), AmortizationRow, LoanStatus, NetWorthClassBreakdown, NetWorthSummary, NetWorthPoint. All money/rate/value are REAL number (L0 decision). These feed Tasks 4-19 (valuation engines, repos, net-worth rollup).
- L2 Web UI: apps/web Vite/React/TS SPA over REST API; read-only /expenses endpoints; build complete on layer/2-web-ui — L2 (Web UI) built 2026-06-15 via subagent-driven-development on branch layer/2-web-ui (off main, ~24 commits). T1 but NO Groww re-validation needed beyond the standing gate (no core financial logic touched — verified golden-master 6/6 unchanged; core 222 + api 45 + web 15 green).

STACK (decided): new apps/web package = Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind + TanStack Query + React Router + Recharts. PURE consumer of the Fastify REST API — no business logic in the browser (all money math stays in core/API). Imports @myfinance/core for shared TYPES only. Vite dev proxies /api/*→http://localhost:${API_PORT:-3001} (no CORS plugin needed); VITE_API_BASE defaults to /api.

ARCHITECTURE (Approach A): feature-folder (features/{networth,investments,expenses,loans,assistant}) + thin typed API client (lib/apiClient.ts {data}-envelope unwrap + ApiError; lib/hooks.ts TanStack Query resource+mutation hooks; lib/queryKeys.ts) + shared UI kit (components/ui/* — Card/KPIStat/Badge(MARKET|COMPUTED|MANUAL)/DataState/AIInsightCard/Modal + Recharts wrappers Trend/Donut/SpendBar). Pure logic isolated + TDD'd in lib/format.ts (INR lakh/crore, compact ₹1.84Cr/₹11.8L, percent, date) and lib/transforms.ts (groupHoldingsByClass, allocationToChartData, summaryByCategoryWithNames).

SCREENS: Net Worth (hero+composition donut+Assets−Liab KPIs+trend), Investments (KPI strip incl XIRR + asset-class group cards w/ valuation badges) + Investment Analyzer (single-holding), Expenses (Spent/Income/Saved KPIs + donut + MoM bars + txn list), Loans (cards + amortization drawer), Assistant placeholder. WRITE FLOWS built: Add-investment (computed/manual per-class form), Add-account, Add-loan (tenure-based), file-import upload (multipart).

ONE API ADDITION (read-only, no Groww re-validation): GET /expenses (filterable from/to/direction/search/categoryId/accountId/limit/offset) + GET /expenses/summary (totalSpent/totalIncome/saved + byCategory + byMonth) over new ExpenseTransactionRepo.query()/summary() repo methods — pure SQL aggregation in the repo layer (L0 seam), debit-only spend grouping; no XIRR/portfolio/categorize/NAV.

KEY DECISIONS: charts use REAL data only — honest empty hints where MF historical NAV is unwired (no fabricated data, faithful-reporting). Budgets DEFERRED (spike §6.9). AI cards static + disabled "Coming in L4" (L4 seam reserved, no agent calls). Testing = TDD on endpoints+lib pure logic + UI-kit smoke test; LIGHT frontend tests + a written manual browser test plan (apps/web/MANUAL_TEST_PLAN.md) — user's explicit choice (verifies UI in-browser via computer-use plugin).

DEFERRED (logged, not built): edit-transaction-category UI (needs a NEW per-transaction category endpoint touching categorization → its own T1); MF historical-NAV wiring (trend-chart follow-up); live stock/gold pricing; Add-loan EMI-mode (tenure-only v1); budgets.

- L2 categorization/rules Expenses UX: editable chips + learn prompt + manage panel; T2, no categorize algorithm change — Added the Expenses categorization/rules UX that L2 had deferred (2026-06-15, T2, same branch layer/2-web-ui / PR #5). Classified T2 (not T1) because it only EXPOSES the existing tested categorization engine via new endpoints + UI — domain/categorize.ts is BYTE-IDENTICAL. T2 gate held: Groww golden-master 6/6 unchanged, core 227 + api 52 + web 15 green, typecheck clean.

BACKEND (additive only): CategoryRepo gained create/rename/delete/exists — DELETE semantics (user-chosen): reassign tagged transactions to category_id=NULL + DELETE dependent category_rules + DELETE category, run as 3 ordered drizzle statements (foreign_keys=ON requires null-out + delete-children BEFORE delete-parent; non-transactional, accepted for low-volume user CRUD). New API: GET /categories/rules (list via getActiveRules), POST /categories {name,icon?} (slugifyCategoryName id, 201, 409 if exists), PATCH/DELETE /categories/:id (404 if missing), PATCH /transactions/:id/category {categoryId:string|null, createRuleMerchant?:boolean} (updateCategory source='manual'; if createRuleMerchant+non-null → extractMerchantKey(description) → saveCategoryMemoryRule(merchant) → recategorizeNonManualTransactions). ExpenseTransactionRepo.getById added (404 + description for merchant-key). GET /categories/rules MUST be registered BEFORE /categories/:id (Fastify path collision). slugifyCategoryName/extractMerchantKey/saveCategoryMemoryRule re-exported from core index.

FRONTEND: CategoryChip per txn (click→category picker→reassign; null categoryId rendered as flagged amber dashed "Uncategorized" = the work-list). Learn prompt: on reassign, one-off assignment fires FIRST (createRuleMerchant absent), THEN inline "Always categorize <merchant> as X?" Yes/No — Yes re-calls with createRuleMerchant:true (creates merchant rule + recategorizes), No dismisses. ManageCategoriesModal: categories CRUD (add/rename/delete-with-confirm) + rules CRUD (add/edit-category/delete) + "Recategorize all" (POST /recategorize). Hooks: useRules/useUpdateTxCategory/useCreateCategory/useRenameCategory/useDeleteCategory/useCreateRule/useUpdateRule/useDeleteRule/useRecategorize.

KEY DESIGN INSIGHT: the categorization engine is DETERMINISTIC rule-matching (no confidence score) — every txn is already auto-categorized at import via the priority ladder merchant_rule(200)>upi_note_keyword(100)>builtin_rule>null, with category_source recording HOW. So the mockup's per-row "✓/✗ is this right?" was DROPPED (would ask you to confirm hundreds of confidently-matched rows); replaced with editable chips on every row + visually-flagged uncategorized rows as the real work-list. extractMerchantKey only derives keys from STRUCTURED bank formats (UPI-/ACH/POS), so the learn prompt only creates a rule when a merchant key is derivable (plain descriptions like "SWIGGY ORDER 123" yield null → no rule, by design). LIVE-VERIFIED end-to-end: UPI-SWIGGY txn → reassign+Yes → rule 'swiggy→food_dining' created → sibling UPI-SWIGGY auto-recategorized.

STILL DEFERRED: auto-rule-creation for non-bank-format descriptions (no merchant key); budgets; MF historical-NAV trend wiring. NEXT after PR #5 merges = L3 (MCP Server). Related decision: c679a0f5 (L2 build).

## Convention
- Tiered SDLC for agent-built work: T1 full discipline / T2 light / T3 direct, with core-logic override and SessionStart-hook triage — Work is triaged by size to save tokens. T1 (layer/substantial feature OR any core-logic change): full discipline — brainstorm spec → writing-plans → branch → TDD → subagent review → PR → update master plan. T2 (scoped, few files): short inline plan → implement → tests → self-review. T3 (localized bug/tweak): direct fix → verify. OVERRIDE: any change touching core financial logic (XIRR, categorization, parsers, portfolio math) is ALWAYS T1 with Groww re-validation, regardless of size (it regresses silently). When unsure, round up a tier. Enforced by a minimal SessionStart hook that injects 'classify tier per myfinance-sdlc skill'; the fat procedure lives in .claude/skills/myfinance-sdlc and lazy-loads. CLAUDE.md holds a backstop pointer. Plugin rejected (YAGNI). MASTER_PLAN.md is durable state, read-first/update-last every session.

## Testing
- L0 core package COMPLETE — Groww golden-master passed (max 0.046pp), Phase 6 API remains — L0 progress checkpoint 2026-06-12. Branch layer/0-foundation, 25 commits past af19bd2. packages/core is COMPLETE: schema+migrations, 7 repos, full domain port (xirr verbatim, returns/portfolio repo-injected, categorize, nav, amfi), import parsers+orchestration, seed + public index.ts. Tests 170/170 green.

T1 CORE-LOGIC GATE PASSED: Groww golden-master (packages/core/test/golden/groww.golden.test.ts, fixture growwGolden.json — anonymized, PII-stripped, committed) validates ported XIRR per-scheme vs Groww's real reported XIRR across 6 schemes / 104 real SIP transactions. Max abs diff 0.046pp, all within 0.5pp tolerance. SCHEME_D matched to 0.001pp. The port is provably faithful to production financial truth. Characterization tests (4.1) also pin the pipeline.

SEAM INVARIANT VERIFIED: no drizzle/better-sqlite3 import anywhere in domain/ or import/ (only architecture comments mention them). Pure logic depends only on repo interfaces.

REMAINING: Phase 6 (Task 6.1 Fastify skeleton+config+errors, 6.2 db plugin+/health, 6.3 read endpoints /transactions + /investments/summary + /investments/returns) then Phase 7 close-out (full verify, subagent final review, PR, update MASTER_PLAN, memory). Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-l0-foundation-design.md; plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-12-l0-foundation.md. Node 20 required for all commands.

## Scope
- Insert L1.5 Unified Investment Model (all asset classes) before L2; build L1 on current MF model first with multi-platform + multi-account seams — User wants the investment section to be a single net-worth view across ALL asset classes (mutual funds, stocks, PPF, FD, cash), each with its own ingestion method (some manual, some file-import), plus multiple accounts per asset class. Decided 2026-06-13:

SEQUENCING: Build L1 (Ingestion + Data API) NOW on the current MF-only model, then insert a NEW layer L1.5 "Unified Investment Model" before L2 (Web UI). Rejected "redesign foundation first" — L1's ingestion PATTERN (multipart HTTP layer + parser registry + normalized ParsedData + transactional writes) survives the generalization, so L1 is proving ground not throwaway, and it keeps a working Groww-validated vertical slice at every step.

L1 SEAMS (baked in now): (1) multi-platform parser registry keyed by `platform` form field — registry maps (platform, kind) -> parser emitting the SAME normalized ParsedData the orchestrators consume; Groww + HDFC initially, ET Money etc. are drop-in modules with zero endpoint/orchestrator change. (2) multi-account first-class — schema already has accountName + investmentApp on every investment table + core getAccounts()/account filter; L1 adds GET /investments/accounts. Multiple Groww MF accounts = different accountName; Groww vs ET Money = different investmentApp.

L1.5 MODEL: asset classes differ on TWO axes — INGESTION (file-import via L1 registry vs manual-entry CRUD; PPF/FD/cash are user-entered) and VALUATION (market = qty x live price API; computed = interest compounding to date for PPF/FD; manual = user-stated). Generalized schema ~= accounts + assets(assetClass) + generalized cashflows + per-class valuation/ingestion strategy + net-worth rollup. Reshapes core schema => T1 with Groww re-validation; migrate existing MF data in, golden-master is safety net. Only investment orchestrators/repos generalize; expense import + L1 parser/HTTP layer carry forward.

ALSO IN L1.5 SCOPE (added 2026-06-13): EXPENSE multi-account. Current expense model (transactions table) has NO account dimension — single implicit account. L1.5 should generalize the `accounts` concept across BOTH expenses and investments so the user can have multiple bank/expense accounts too, not just multiple investment accounts.
- L1 Ingestion spec + plan complete and committed; build not started — resume by executing the TDD plan — L1 (Ingestion + Data API) T1 brainstorm + planning phase DONE 2026-06-13, on main (docs-only commits 1538739, 5493ef5, 98af099, ad60b75, 916d619). NO code written yet — only docs. Resume in a new session by executing the plan.

SPEC: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-l1-ingestion-design.md. PLAN: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-13-l1-ingestion.md (10 TDD tasks, fully self-contained — exact file paths, code, commands, expected output). MASTER_PLAN L1 row links both + §8 has a "RESUME HERE" pointer.

WHAT L1 BUILDS (packages/api HTTP layer over core, no new package): 3 multipart import endpoints (POST /imports/expenses, /imports/investments/holdings, /imports/investments/transactions) behind a multi-platform PARSER REGISTRY (src/import/registry.ts, resolveParser(platform,kind); hdfc+groww now); GET /imports; expanded reads GET /investments/holdings|allocation|accounts (multi-account D7); categories GET + rule CRUD (POST/PATCH/DELETE /categories/rules) + POST /recategorize. Seams: txRunner (sqlite.transaction), injectable amfiMatch via buildServer({amfiMatch}) for offline tests, reuse L0 NavLookup. unmatched_schemes returned as 200 payload (D2). One non-financial core add: ImportHistoryRepo.listAll().

EXECUTION: use superpowers:subagent-driven-development, fresh subagent per task, start Task 0 (branch layer/1-ingestion off main + add @fastify/multipart@^9). GOTCHAS baked into plan: Node 20 mandatory (source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 20); typecheck via `tsc --build` not --noEmit (api->core project ref); fixtures generated programmatically with xlsx (test/fixtures/makeFixtures.ts) to match parser header-alias detection, PII-free, with a parse-sanity gate before building routes; @fastify/multipart NOT yet installed; push/PR needs `gh auth switch --user ak688744`.

NEXT AFTER L1: L1.5 Unified Investment Model (see decision 437133fd).
- NEXT SESSION: run a standalone frontend/platform DESIGN SPIKE (visual mockups) before L1.5 — feeds L1.5 + L2 specs — Decided 2026-06-13 (right after L1 completed, PR #2 open). Before building L1.5, do ONE round of frontend/platform design as a STANDALONE design spike — NOT a build. Rationale: L1.5 is the expensive layer (reshapes core schema to all asset classes + first-class accounts, T1 WITH Groww re-validation); the best input to that schema is what the UI needs to show (net-worth rollup, how PPF/FD/cash are entered-vs-imported, per-asset insight cards, multi-account switching, where loans live). Discovering a missing data dimension from a mockup is cheap; discovering it post-migration is not. This extends the principle already in the L1 spec (enriched expense reads were deferred to L2 'so the API is shaped by real intuitive-UI needs rather than guessed').

USER CHOICES for the spike (via AskUserQuestion): (1) SEQUENCING = "Standalone design spike now" — a dedicated UX/design brainstorm producing mockups + a design doc whose output becomes a NAMED INPUT to the later L1.5 spec and L2; PR #2 can merge in parallel. (2) FIDELITY = "Real visual mockups" — use the Figma/Stitch design tools (mcp__stitch__*, mcp__design__figma__*) to produce actual rendered screens, escalating cost accepted. NOT just ASCII wireframes.

SCOPE GUARDRAILS: This is the brainstorm phase only — output is mockups + a committed design doc at docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md. NO code, NO core changes, NO building L2. Terminal state of the brainstorm is NOT writing-plans (that belongs inside the actual L1.5/L2 layers later) — it's a design doc that informs those future specs. Keep it a spike: if it drifts into 'let's build the React app', stop. Surface new platform ideas (net-worth trend, account comparison, loans placement) so they land in the data model BEFORE the L1.5 foundation is poured.

COVERAGE the spike should explore: unified investment / net-worth dashboard across all asset classes (MF, stocks, PPF, FD, cash); multi-account views + switching (D7 already in L1; L1.5 generalizes to expenses too); manual-entry vs file-import ingestion UX per asset class; per-asset insight cards; expense views (enriched reads were deferred to L2 precisely to design against concrete layouts); where loans fit. The AI/agent layer (L4) is the eventual product — keep a place for 'chat with your data' / wealth-manager insights in the IA.

HOW TO START NEW SESSION: SessionStart hook fires -> read MASTER_PLAN -> this is a design/UX task (T1-ish new surface but design-only; classify per myfinance-sdlc, likely treat as a brainstorm spike, confirm tier with user). Invoke superpowers:brainstorming. The brainstorming skill was already launched at the end of the prior session but the user chose to restart fresh — re-run it cleanly. Pending: also merge PR #2 (https://github.com/ak688744/MyFinance/pull/2) to main when ready (gh auth switch --user ak688744), then L1.5 proper after the design spike. Related decision: L1 complete id ab012766; L1.5 model/sequencing id 437133fd.
- Frontend/platform design spike complete (PR #3 merged) — IA + 6 mockups + L1.5 schema inputs locked — The standalone frontend/platform design spike (decided in c5a4e601) is DONE and merged to main as PR #3 (branch design/frontend-spike). Brainstorm-phase-only: no code, no core changes, did NOT proceed to writing-plans. Deliverable = docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-13-frontend-design-spike-design.md + 6 rendered mockups in specs/mockups/ (01 networth-home, 02 investments, 03 investment-analyzer, 04 add-asset-fd, 05 expenses, 06 loans; + inspiration-expenses.png reference). Mockups built with Stitch (project 5629957510051795265, design system asset 2726046840844504940) and rendered crisply via headless Chrome.

APPROVED IA ("three pillars + AI", Option A): left sidebar Net Worth · Investments · Expenses · Loans · Assistant. Chosen because it maps 1:1 onto the L1.5 asset/liability schema AND the L4 agent split (investment/expense/loan specialists under a wealth-manager orchestrator).

PRODUCT IDENTITY: net-worth command-center (landing = one net-worth number + trend + asset-class composition + assets−liabilities; NOT chat-first, NOT a section launcher). Asset classes tracked: MF, stocks, PPF, EPF, NPS, FD, gold, real estate, cash (crypto/bonds deferred, enum stays open). Liabilities first-class (loans w/ EMI/rate/tenure/amortization) netted into net worth. Desktop-rendered, responsive; web first.

KEY OUTPUTS FOR L1.5 SCHEMA (doc §6 = the payload):
1. assetClass open enum.
2. valuationStrategy = market | computed | manual, VISIBLE in UI (badges + 'Updated N days ago' freshness chip for manual; manual needs lastValuedAt).
3. ingestionMode = file_import (L1 parser registry) | manual_entry (per-class CRUD form), orthogonal to valuation.
4. First-class accounts SCOPED PER-DOMAIN (per asset class / expense-vs-investment), NOT one global account list — DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE from the MASTER_PLAN L1.5 note that mused a single unified accounts model. L1.5 should model accounts per-domain.
5. First-class liabilities; net worth = Σ asset values − Σ liability outstanding.
6. Per-class computed inputs (FD: principal/rate/compounding/start/maturity/payout; PPF/EPF/NPS: balance/rate/contributions; gold: grams+stated; real estate: stated; loan: principal/rate/tenure/EMI→amortization).
7. AI insight slot as a data concern (text+severity+CTA) shared by UI cards and L4 agents.
8. NEW DIMENSION — historical net-worth / valuation snapshots: hero trend + per-class trend + loan payoff projection all need time series, absent from the MF-only model. L1.5 must decide snapshot-table vs derive-from-cashflows+price-history. (Clearest 'found a missing dimension in a mockup' win.)
9. NEW — budgets (Expenses shows '% of budget'/'Budget left'): per-category/overall monthly budget; L1.5 decides core vs API/UI.

PLATFORM PATTERN: AI is EMBEDDED + ON-DEMAND, not a persistent side panel — each screen has a contextual collapsible AI insight card + a floating 'Ask AI' button opening a chat drawer; drill-downs embed scoped 'ask a follow-up'; Assistant nav = full L4 home. (Decided during the Expenses iteration after the user rejected a persistent right rail as wasted space.) Expenses screen was iterated against a user-provided reference; final = full-width, KPI strip, inline collapsible AI insight, donut + simple month-on-month spend BAR chart (user rejected multi-line trend), recent txns with editable category chips + per-row account tags + inline auto-categorize 'correct?' affordance.

DESIGN SYSTEM intent for L2: calm/data-dense ('Bloomberg-calm meets modern fintech'); primary blue #1463F3; green #0E9F6E/red #E02424 ONLY for financial deltas; violet #7C5CFC reserved for AI; Manrope headings + Inter body, tabular numerals; ₹ lakh/crore formatting; white 12px cards on #F7F8FA.

STATE: L0+L1+spike all merged to main. NEXT = L1.5 Unified Investment Model as fresh T1 (Groww re-validation), using doc §6 as schema input and §3–§5 as L2 UI contract. Related: c5a4e601 (spike decision), 437133fd (L1.5 model/sequencing), ab012766 (L1 complete).

KEY GOTCHAS for subagents: (1) Node 20 mandatory — prefix cmds `source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 20 && ...`. (2) repo methods are SYNCHRONOUS (better-sqlite3) — domain port must drop await; only NAV (fetch) stays async. (3) Drizzle select() is UNORDERED — any logic relying on natural/chronological row order MUST add explicit ORDER BY (caught a real unit-accumulation regression in 2.2). (4) seed parent investment_schemes rows before investment_transactions in tests (foreign_keys=ON).

## Architecture
- undefined
- Task 3.4: navService ported verbatim to packages/core/src/domain/nav/; amfiMatcher pure scoring verbatim, autoMatchAmfiCodes repo-injected — navService is already a pure global-fetch client (cache/TTL/DD-MM-YYYY parsing preserved exactly). amfiMatcher.autoMatchAmfiCodes no longer takes a db+SQL; takes deps:{schemeRepo}. Added SchemeRepo.getUnmatchedSchemes() (SELECT WHERE amfi_code IS NULL ORDER BY scheme_name) for faithfulness to the original SELECT rather than filtering getSchemes(). force option accepted for parity but ignored (no bulk-clear in repo layer).
- core package public surface: index.ts barrel; ImportTransactionsResult collision resolved by renaming the importInvestmentTransactions variant to ImportInvestmentTransactionsResult on re-export — importTransactions.ts and importInvestmentTransactions.ts both export ImportTransactionsResult with different shapes; TransactionType is identical in types.ts and transactionParser.ts so re-exported from types only. seedDatabase(db) ports the RN initializeDatabase seed step (upsertStarter all starterCategories, then recategorizeNonManualTransactions), synchronous + idempotent via INSERT OR IGNORE.

## Validation
- Groww golden-master gate passes: ported XIRR/returns math reproduces real per-scheme Groww XIRR within 0.046pp (tolerance 0.5pp) for all 6 schemes — Core-logic T1 validation gate for L0 Foundation. Per-scheme getPeriodReturns(ALL) with NAV stub (currentNav as terminal) and today=asOfDate. Max diff 0.046pp (SCHEME_A), confirms the L3 returns.ts port is faithful to the RN original. No domain code modified; tolerance untouched. Commit 9df1a82.

## L1-api-multipart
- First-file-wins multipart helper with ArrayBuffer cast — L1 Task 2: readMultipart() implements first-file-wins by checking if(file===null) before assigning, and drains ignored file parts via toBuffer(). All text fields collected even after first file. nodeBuffer.buffer.slice() requires 'as ArrayBuffer' cast for TypeScript (SharedArrayBuffer vs ArrayBuffer union), but runtime is unchanged. @fastify/multipart augmentation loaded via import. Typecheck clean. Commit 9df4136.

## L1.5-build
- L1.5 plan Task 4 computed-valuation test had a leap-year day-count defect; resolved by fixing test expected-values to spec's days/365 convention, implementation kept verbatim — The plan's Task 4 test hard-coded whole-year exponents (Math.pow(1.02,4)) and 730-day spans, but the spec prose mandates yearsBetween=days/365 and 2024 is a leap year (366 days; 2023-01-01..2025-01-01=731 days). Verbatim test vs verbatim impl were mutually inconsistent for any window spanning Feb 2024. Resolution (a): keep computed.ts exactly as specified (days/365, the Groww-validated XIRR day-count), correct only the test EXPECTED values to 366/365, 731/365 etc. Preserves closed-form property; financial behavior unchanged. Task 16's test already used 366/365 correctly, confirming day-counting was the intended convention. All 7 computed tests green.

## L1.5-complete
- L1.5 Unified Investment Model BUILT and shipped as PR #4 (branch layer/1.5-unified-investment); MF pipeline untouched, Groww golden-master unchanged — Executed the 30-task TDD plan via subagent-driven-development. Delivered: 6 new core tables (accounts/assets/asset_contributions/asset_rates/asset_valuations/liabilities) + nullable transactions.account_id (migration 0001_round_glorian.sql); valuation engine domain/valuation/ (rate-period compound interest FD/PPF/EPF/NPS, manual stated-value+ageDays, valueAsset dispatcher); domain/loans/amortization.ts (computeEmi/amortizationSchedule/loanStatus); domain/networth/networth.ts (getAllAssets read-time union projection of untouched MF holdings + generic assets, getNetWorth=Σassets-Σliab, getNetWorthHistory derive-on-read); 6 repos; expense account_id+updateAccount; API routes /accounts /liabilities /assets(+sub) /networth(+/history); MF import ensureAccount (non-financial). T1 GATE PASSED: Groww golden-master 6/6 unchanged, core 215 + api 42 green, typecheck clean tsc --build, seam invariant clean, rollup-continuity (MF-only getNetWorth.totalAssets==getPortfolioSummary.totalCurrentValue) + textbook-oracle math. Code review READY TO MERGE. GOTCHAS: (1) plan Task-4 test had leap-year day-count defect (whole-year exponents vs spec days/365); fixed TEST expected values to 366/365 & 731/365, impl verbatim. (2) loanStatus boundary: payment due exactly today = next due not paid (dueDate<today paid, >=today remaining). (3) extending ExpenseTransactionRepo.insertIgnore with required accountId broke importTransactions caller + 2 existing tests + categorize fake repo — all updated (accountId:null for expense, which has no account dimension yet). NEXT=merge PR #4 then L2 Web UI.

## Bug fixes

BUG-005 GET /liabilities returned emiAmount=null for tenure-based loans; fix: enrich list with computed emi via core computeEmi (no core change). BUG-002 InvestmentsPage double-counted MF (both /holdings and /assets project MF); fix: groupHoldingsByClass skips assetClass==mutual_fund generic assets. BUG-001 KPI strip MF-only; fix: relabel as MF portfolio + add all-assets total KPI. BUG-006 secondary error cards lacked Retry; fix: onRetry on Investments holdings/assets + Expenses recent txns. BUG-008 no expense add/import entry; fix: +Import expenses button launches ImportModal (defaults expenses). BUG-004 seed/expectation: swiggy@axis-order has no builtin food keyword by design; fix test plan seed to -breakfast + clarifying note (no core change). BUG-007 visual parity deferred per user.

## How to Save Decisions (for Copilot)
When the user makes an architectural decision, tech stack choice, convention, or
discusses a constraint during this session, save it by appending a JSON line to
`.ai-memory/decisions.jsonl` in this exact format:
```json
{"id":"<8-char-random-hex>","ts":"<ISO8601-timestamp>","category":"architecture|constraint|convention|testing|scope|unresolved","decision":"<one clear sentence>","rationale":"<why this was decided>","confidence":1.0,"source":"copilot"}
```
After saving, regenerate this file by reading ALL decisions from `.ai-memory/decisions.jsonl`,
grouping by category, and reproducing this exact format including this instruction section.

## Research Memory

BEFORE investigating APIs, libraries, errors, or patterns:
1. Read `.ai-memory/research.jsonl` and search topic/tags for your query
2. If stable match found → use directly
3. If versioned match → check version_anchored against current; use if same, re-research if different
4. If volatile match → use as hypothesis, verify
5. If no match → proceed with research, then save finding

### How to Save Research Findings (for copilot)
When you discover API behavior, library compatibility, error root causes, performance findings,
documentation clarifications, or workarounds, save it by appending a JSON line to
`.ai-memory/research.jsonl` in this exact format:
```json
{"id":"<8-char-random-hex>","ts":"<ISO8601-timestamp>","topic":"<5-15 word noun phrase>","tags":["<1-5 keywords>"],"finding":"<concise description of what was found>","source_tool":"copilot","source_context":"<what prompted this research>","confidence":<0.0-1.0>,"staleness":"stable|versioned|volatile","supersedes":null,"version_anchored":null}
```
Staleness guide: "stable" = won't change (language behavior, math), "versioned" = tied to library version, "volatile" = may change anytime (API responses, service behavior).

## Auto-Save Rules (always active)

- **Decisions**: When a project decision is made during this session, immediately save it to `.ai-memory/decisions.jsonl`. Do not ask — save automatically and briefly confirm.
- **Research**: When you discover API behavior, library details, error root causes, workarounds, or performance findings, immediately save to `.ai-memory/research.jsonl`. Do not ask — save automatically and briefly confirm.
- **Search first**: Before investigating any API, library, error, or technical pattern, first search `.ai-memory/research.jsonl` for existing findings.
